name: Roasted Butternut Squash, Rosemary, and Garlic Lasagne
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds butternut squash, quartered, seeded, peeled, and cut into 1/2-inch dice (about 9 1/2 cups)
9 sheets dry no-boil lasagne pasta (7 x 3 1/2-inch)
1 1/3 cups freshly grated Parmesan cheese (about 5 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F. and oil two large shallow baking pans.
2. In a large bowl, toss squash with oil until coated well and spread in one layer in pans. Roast squash in the oven for 10 minutes and season with salt. Stir squash and roast for an additional 10 to 15 minutes, or until tender and beginning to turn golden.
3. While the squash is roasting, bring milk to a simmer with rosemary in a saucepan over low heat. Heat the milk mixture through a sieve into a large pitcher or measuring cup.
4. In a large heavy saucepan, cook garlic in butter over moderately low heat, stirring until softened. Stir in flour and cook the roux, stirring for 3 minutes. Remove pan from heat and whisk in the milk mixture slowly until smooth. Return the pan to heat and simmer the sauce, whisking occasionally, for about 10 minutes, or until thick.
5. Stir in squash and season with salt and pepper to taste. The sauce may be made up to 3 days ahead and chilled, its surface covered with plastic wrap.
6. Reduce temperature to 375 degrees F. and butter a baking dish, 13 x 9 x 2 inches.
7. Pour 1 cup of sauce into the baking dish (the sauce will not cover the bottom completely) and cover with 3 lasagne sheets, making sure they do not touch each other.
8. Spread half of the remaining sauce over the pasta and sprinkle with 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese. Make one more layer in the same manner, beginning and ending with pasta.
9. In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat cream with salt until it holds soft peaks and spread evenly over the top pasta layer, making sure the pasta is completely covered. Sprinkle remaining 1/3 cup Parmesan cheese over cream. Cover dish tightly with foil, tenting slightly to prevent the foil from touching the top layer, and bake in the middle of the oven for 30 minutes.
10. Remove foil and bake lasagne for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is bubbling and golden. Let lasagne stand for 5 minutes.
11. Garnish each serving with fresh rosemary sprigs.
Yield: 6 servings as a main course or 12 as a side dish
